summary: Q1 freight cost rose 9% after a port closure; recommend temporary rerouting and buffer stock.

blocks.content: On-time delivery dropped from 96% to 91% and backlog rose 14%; operations expects a six-week recovery.

source_span.quote: On-time delivery dropped from 96% to 91% over the quarter, while backlog increased by 14%. Operations estimates a six-week recovery once rerouting is in place.

blocks.content: Freight: +9% • On-time: 96%→91% • Backlog: +14% • Recovery est.: 6 weeks

source_span.quote:
Q1 freight cost rose 9% after a port closure on the west coast.

On-time delivery dropped from 96% to 91% over the quarter, while backlog increased by 14%. Operations estimates a six-week recovery once rerouting is in place.

blocks.content: Closure followed a labour dispute at the primary port; lasted 12 days; 10 of 14 weekly shipments delayed by avg 4.5 days.

source_span.quote: The closure followed a labour dispute at the primary port and lasted twelve days. During that period, ten of our fourteen weekly shipments were delayed by an average of 4.5 days.

blocks.content: Approve temporary rerouting and a two-week buffer-stock build for top-30 SKUs.

source_span.quote: Approve a temporary rerouting through alternate ports and a two-week buffer-stock build for top-30 SKUs.

blocks.content: Recommendation rests on Q1 data alone; reassess in six weeks before structural changes.

source_span.quote: This recommendation is based on Q1 data alone; a six-week reassessment is required before structural changes.
